experiment: Bit-floor: scan fewer 1-bit dims to raise QPS. binary + rotate2 + residual, scan-bits sweep x C, Cohere 1M. (Task #3)
note: NEGATIVE as a QPS play. 4x fewer scan words (1024->256 bits) buys only ~+8% QPS (1028->1114 at C=200) while recall COLLAPSES (0.995->0.72 at C=500). The scan is NOT the batch bottleneck at these N/C - the rerank (C f32 gathers) + heap dominate - so shrinking the scan barely moves total QPS, and residual cannot hold recall at 256b for 1M (too much competition). The 049/050 bandwidth-wall QPS gain does not materialize because rerank, not scan, is the cost at C>=200. Confirms 051 (fewer bits = latency not QPS). Bit-floor is a dead end for QPS; the lever is NOT fewer bits.
